description: Figure 7 presents Mott–Schottky curves (C⁻² versus potential, V_SCE) for Fe–30Mn–8Al–1C–xCu low-density steels with 0, 1, and 3 wt% Cu tested in 3.5 wt% NaCl solution. The plot is divided into three regions: Region I (approximately −1.2 to −0.5 V) shows positive slopes indicating n-type semiconductor behavior of the passive film; Region II (−0.5 to −0.2 V) exhibits a peak representing the transition zone; and Region III (> −0.2 V) displays negative slopes characteristic of p-type semiconductor behavior. The curves reveal that increasing Cu content progressively raises the C⁻² values in the linear regions. The caption states that the calculated donor (N_D) and acceptor (N_A) densities increase from 0Cu to 3Cu, indicating a higher concentration of point defects in the passive film with Cu addition. This trend supports the finding that Cu alloying degrades the protective quality of the passive film, consistent with the observed deterioration of corrosion resistance—particularly the severe grain-boundary microgalvanic corrosion in the 3Cu steel.
